title: UI Design Pre-stage — invoke /ui-ux-pro-max for Dashboard 3-view design system
description: Pre-stage design task: invoke the /ui-ux-pro-max skill with specifications for the 3 Dashboard views (rules-tree, human-lock, intent-timeline). Produce ui-design/ folder containing: tokens.json (design tokens for Preact/Vite SPA), rules-tree.html (interactive tree prototype), human-lock.html (audit card list + approve button prototype), intent-timeline.html (dual-column AI|Human timeline prototype), and a component-inventory.md listing reusable component names, props, and visual states. The D5 Preact SPA implementation depends on these artifacts.

files.change: Design tokens: color palette (dark maintenance-tool aesthetic), typography scale, spacing system, component state colors (locked/drift/pending/approved), exported as CSS custom properties compatible with Preact/Vite
Interactive HTML prototype: tree-view of AgentsMeta.nodes (L0/L1/L2 hierarchy), human_locked_nearby highlight (amber border), stale badge (red dot), expand/collapse nodes, responsive layout

Interactive HTML prototype: card list of HumanLockEntry items, drift status indicator (hash mismatch = orange warning), approve button per card, confirmed = green checkmark, file path + line range display
Interactive HTML prototype: dual-column layout (AI | Human), entries sorted by timestamp descending, diff_stat badge (±N lines), annotation button on human column entries, source tag chip (ai/human)

files.target: Component inventory for TASK-009 Preact implementation
files.change: List of reusable components: TreeNode, LockCard, TimelineEntry, SourceBadge, DriftIndicator, ApproveButton — each with props interface, visual states, and animation hints

implementation: Invoke /ui-ux-pro-max skill with the following 3 view specifications derived from discussion.md Round 3:

View 1 — Rules Tree Browser: tree-render of AgentsMeta.nodes hierarchy (L0 = project constitution, L1 = domain groups, L2 = file-level rules); highlight human_locked_nearby in amber; stale badge when drift detected; collapsible nodes

View 2 — Human Lock Vault: card list per HumanLockEntry; each card shows file path + line range + locked content preview; drift state = orange warning + hash mismatch detail; Approve button posts to POST /api/human-lock/approve

View 3 — Intent Timeline: dual-column AI | Human sorted by ts desc; AI column shows commit_sha + diff_stat + rule_ref; Human column shows annotation text + approve timestamp; Annotate button opens inline text input posting to POST /api/intent/annotate
Design constraint: maintenance-tool aesthetic (not consumer-app) — monospace elements, muted palette, information-dense without clutter

reference.examples: View specifications from discussion.md Round 3 monorepo structure section (rules-tree.tsx, human-lock.tsx, intent-timeline.tsx descriptions)

criteria: ui-design/ folder contains all 5 artifacts: tokens.json, rules-tree.html, human-lock.html, intent-timeline.html, component-inventory.md
Each HTML prototype opens in browser and renders without JavaScript errors
tokens.json is valid JSON containing color, typography, and spacing tokens as CSS custom properties
component-inventory.md lists at minimum 6 components with props and visual states
HTML prototypes visually demonstrate: tree hierarchy in rules-tree.html, drift+approve interaction in human-lock.html, dual-column layout in intent-timeline.html

rationale.chosen_approach: Pre-stage UI design before Preact implementation ensures D5 has clear component contract to implement against, reducing back-and-forth during coding
alternatives_considered: Design inline with D5 implementation (risks visual inconsistency)
Skip prototyping and go straight to code (loses UX validation opportunity)
decision_factors: User decision: UI design pre-stage via /ui-ux-pro-max
D5 implementation must depend on these artifacts to know component props and visual states
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds a pre-stage task before Wave B but reduces TASK-009 implementation ambiguity

manual_checks: Open all 3 HTML prototypes in browser — verify they render without console errors
Verify tokens.json is valid JSON with at least 3 token categories (color, typography, spacing)
Review component-inventory.md — verify it contains enough detail for TASK-009 Preact implementation
success_metrics: All 3 HTML prototypes render without JS errors

risks.description: ui-ux-pro-max skill produces designs incompatible with Preact component model
risks.mitigation: component-inventory.md explicitly lists props as TypeScript interfaces compatible with Preact; TASK-009 implementer adapts as needed
risks.fallback: If prototypes are too complex, TASK-009 can simplify to minimal viable views
